prompt: An intelligent document processing pipeline with Azure AI Document Intelligence for OCR, Azure OpenAI for summarization, Cognitive Search for indexing, Cosmos DB for metadata, and Blob Storage for document retention

services.name: Storage Account
services.description: Stores original and processed document blobs for long-term retention

services.name: Azure Functions
services.description: Serverless orchestrator that coordinates document ingestion, AI processing, and persistence

services.name: Document Intelligence
services.description: Performs OCR and structured data extraction from documents

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.description: Generates concise summaries and insights from extracted document text

services.name: Azure Cognitive Search
services.description: Indexes document content and metadata to enable full-text and semantic search

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.description: Stores document metadata, extracted fields, and summaries

services.name: Microsoft Entra ID
services.description: Provides identity, access tokens, and RBAC for the serverless processing components

services.name: Key Vault
services.description: Securely stores secrets, keys, and connection strings for AI and data services

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.description: Central monitoring service collecting metrics and traces from the orchestration layer

services.name: Log Analytics
services.description: Aggregates and queries logs and telemetry from Azure Monitor for observability

connections.label: Trigger serverless function when a new document blob is uploaded
Submit uploaded document for OCR and layout-aware extraction
Return extracted text and structured fields to orchestrator
Send extracted text to generate a concise summary and key insights
Return generated summary and insights to orchestration logic
Persist document metadata, extracted fields, and summaries
Push searchable content and metadata for indexing
Index document blobs as an external data source for content retrieval
Write processed document versions or annotations back to blob storage
Issue access tokens for secure invocation of downstream Azure services
Provide secrets, API keys, and connection strings at runtime
Emit processing metrics, custom logs, and dependency telemetry
Ingest and store monitoring data for querying and alerts

workflow.description: A new document is uploaded to blob storage, which triggers the serverless orchestration to start processing.
Azure Functions obtains the necessary credentials and configuration to call downstream services securely.
The orchestrator sends the document to Azure AI Document Intelligence for OCR and structured data extraction, then receives the extracted content.
Azure Functions forwards the extracted text to Azure OpenAI to generate a summary and insights, then collects the generated output.
The orchestrator writes document metadata, extracted fields, and summaries into Azure Cosmos DB for fast retrieval and auditing.
Azure Functions submits the searchable content and metadata to Azure Cognitive Search and may store processed document variants back into blob storage.
Azure Cognitive Search connects to blob storage as an external source to index document blobs alongside metadata for rich search.
During each processing run, Azure Functions sends metrics and logs to Azure Monitor, which forwards them into Log Analytics for analysis and alerting.

services.name: Azure Cognitive Search

services.name: Key Vault

services.name: Azure Monitor

services.name: Log Analytics

connections.label: Issue/validate OAuth tokens for callers accessing upload and query APIs
Accept document upload and write raw file to Blob container
Invoke ingestion endpoint to start processing for the uploaded blob URI
Retrieve API keys/connection secrets for AI services and data stores
Submit blob URI for OCR and layout extraction; receive extracted text/fields
Send extracted text to generate document summary and key takeaways
Upsert metadata, processing status, extracted fields, and summary results
Push enriched content and metadata to search index for retrieval
Proxy end-user search queries and return ranked results

Emit function execution metrics, traces, and dependency telemetry
Route collected telemetry to centralized Log Analytics workspace

workflow.description: User authenticates and obtains access token to call the ingestion APIs.
Client uploads a document; API stores the raw file for retention.
API triggers the processing orchestrator with the uploaded blob URI.
Orchestrator retrieves necessary secrets/keys to call downstream services.
Document Intelligence performs OCR/layout extraction for the stored document.
Azure OpenAI summarizes extracted text and produces key insights.
Processing results are persisted as document metadata and status.
Enriched content is indexed to enable full-text search and filtering.
Users query documents; API returns search results and metadata details.
Operational telemetry from processing is collected and centralized for troubleshooting and alerts.

Publish blob-created event when a new document lands in retention container

workflow.description: A document is uploaded to Blob storage and an event is generated for processing.
Event Grid triggers the orchestration function for the specific uploaded document.
The function authenticates with Microsoft Entra ID before invoking downstream services.
The function sends the document to Document Intelligence and receives OCR plus structured extraction results.
Extracted text is sent to Azure OpenAI to produce a summary and key insights.
Processed content is indexed in Azure Cognitive Search for fast semantic and keyword retrieval.
Metadata and processing status are stored in Cosmos DB, and enriched artifacts are saved back to Blob storage.
Pipeline telemetry is sent to Azure Monitor and retained in Log Analytics for operational visibility.

workflow.description: A user signs in to the web application and starts a new document submission session.
The application sends the uploaded file to the orchestration layer, which stores the original document for long-term retention.
The orchestration layer submits the retained document to Document Intelligence and receives OCR text and structured extraction results.
The extracted text is sent to Azure OpenAI to generate a concise summary and normalized insights for downstream use.
The orchestration layer saves metadata, status, summary, and storage references in Cosmos DB.
The orchestration layer publishes extracted content and summaries into Azure Cognitive Search for retrieval.
Users query the application, which retrieves ranked search hits from Cognitive Search and metadata from Cosmos DB.
Processing metrics and traces are emitted from the workflow and stored for operational monitoring and troubleshooting.

workflow.description: User uploads document to Blob Storage; Blob trigger invokes Azure Function.
Function retrieves secrets from Key Vault and authenticates via Microsoft Entra ID.
Function sends document to Document Intelligence for OCR and extraction; receives structured data.
Function sends extracted text to Azure OpenAI for summarization; receives summary.
Function stores metadata, extracted fields, and summary in Cosmos DB.
Function indexes the content and metadata in Cognitive Search (or Cosmos DB change feed triggers indexer).
Function logs metrics and diagnostics to Azure Monitor, which forwards to Log Analytics.
End users query Cognitive Search to retrieve documents and summaries.
